variables.content: The kinematic maps mark free fall as prominent in the collapsed sector and the observed 28 May 2015 event was a vertical fall from an overhang, giving a coherent empirical instance linking mapped free-fall predisposition to an observed free-fall event.
variables.title: Observed event and kinematic maps coherently indicate free fall as a dominant mechanism in the collapsed sector

variables.content: The falling material is treated as a single, coherent vertical column whose center‑of‑mass vertical motion following release is governed by ideal free‑fall kinematics under the standard gravitational acceleration $g=9.80665\ \mathrm{m/s^2}$ with negligible initial vertical velocity and with negligible internal frictional retardation, inter‑particle buoyancy, or drag effects from void fluids; under these assumptions the vertical position is $z(t)=z_0-\tfrac{1}{2}g t^2$ during the interval between release and impact.
variables.title: Free‑fall kinematics for falling rubblized material

paper.en_title: Microseismicity induced by a controlled, mine collapse at White Pine, Michigan

variables.content: It is important to know whether the gas-phase free-fall prior to water entry materially changes key observables (jet speed in water and debris-size statistics) because if free-fall has only a small effect, then prior datasets with free-fall may be more broadly applicable to IVR-ERVC scenarios. Conversely, if free-fall strongly alters breakup and explosion propensity, then distinct experimental datasets for the no-free-fall regime are required. Previous numerical modeling (TRACER-II) has predicted small differences in leading-edge velocity for some geometries, but lacked broad experimental confirmation. Without experimental comparison between no-free-fall and free-fall cases, uncertainty remains in whether existing free-fall datasets can serve as proxies for IVR-ERVC.

paper.en_title: Fuel–Coolant Interaction Visualization Test for In-Vessel Corium Retention External Reactor Vessel Cooling (IVR-ERVC) Condition

variables.content: The calibration apparatus uses a thin Plexiglas plate with a small-diameter hole and a short free-fall distance, specifically chosen to minimize velocity-distribution broadening in the free-fall stream so that the computed free-fall solids fraction (from measured mass flow and computed fall velocity) is a reliable reference for the probe signal.
variables.title: Calibration apparatus design minimizing free-fall velocity distribution error

paper.en_title: Voidage profiles in magnetically fluidized beds

variables.content: For a flat-falling A4 sheet dropped a height $H\approx 1\,\mathrm{m}$ with total fall time $T\approx 1.2\ \mathrm{s}$, the interval during which the vertical acceleration $\mathrm{d}v/\mathrm{d}t$ is non-negligible is short enough that the terminal force balance condition $\mathrm{d}v/\mathrm{d}t\approx 0$ and $m_{s}g\approx F_{\text{air}}(v_{\text{steady}})$ determines the observed descent speed during most of the $1\,\mathrm{m}$ drop.
variables.title: Steady-state force balance applicable for most of a 1 m descent

paper.en_title: Air density measurement with a falling A4 sheet
